name: Vernon
notes: Almost entirely industrial. 50,000+ workers, ~100 residents. Heavy manufacturing, food processing, metal finishing.

name: Commerce
notes: Major industrial city. Large warehouse and distribution hub. Adjacent to Vernon. Citadel Outlets area.

name: Carson
primary_waterbodies: Long Beach Harbor / San Pedro Bay
notes: Significant industrial and refinery activity. Dominguez Channel runs through the city. Near ports of LA/Long Beach.

name: Torrance
notes: Mixed industrial and residential. Major refinery (PBF Energy). Aerospace manufacturing. Coastal zone in western portion.

name: Irwindale
notes: Mining and aggregate extraction. Ready-mix concrete operations. Miller Brewing. Rock quarries throughout city.

name: Santa Fe Springs
notes: Major industrial city. Oil production, manufacturing, distribution. Heritage Falls and Los Nietos area.

name: Long Beach
primary_waterbodies: Long Beach Harbor / San Pedro Bay
notes: Port of Long Beach. Oil production (THUMS islands). Major industrial, port logistics, and coastal zone. Concrete-lined LA River terminus.

name: Compton
notes: Mixed residential and industrial. Compton Creek is 303(d) listed. Light manufacturing and warehousing.

name: Los Angeles
notes: Largest city in county. Spans multiple watersheds. Industrial areas in downtown, San Fernando Valley, Harbor area. Coastal zone along Santa Monica/Venice/San Pedro.

name: Pasadena
notes: Arroyo Seco tributary to LA River. Mix of commercial, institutional (Caltech, JPL), and light industrial.

name: Glendale
notes: Commercial and light industrial. DreamWorks Animation, Disney offices. Verdugo Wash is concrete-lined tributary to LA River.

name: Burbank
notes: Entertainment industry hub (Warner Bros, Disney). Light manufacturing, media production. LA River runs along southern boundary.

name: Santa Monica
notes: Coastal city. Ballona Creek watershed. Coastal zone applies citywide. Tourism, tech, commercial.

name: Beverly Hills
notes: Primarily commercial and residential. Limited industrial. Ballona Creek watershed.

name: Culver City
notes: Media and tech hub (Amazon Studios, Sony). Ballona Creek runs through the city. Hayden Tract industrial area.

name: West Hollywood
notes: Small, dense city. Primarily commercial and residential. Very limited industrial activity.

name: Inglewood
notes: SoFi Stadium, The Forum. Mixed commercial and residential. Some light industrial near airport.

name: Hawthorne
notes: SpaceX headquarters. Aerospace and manufacturing. Near LAX. Dominguez Channel watershed.

name: Gardena
notes: Light manufacturing, auto repair, small industrial shops. South Bay location.

name: Lawndale
notes: Small South Bay city. Primarily residential with some commercial. Dominguez Channel watershed.

name: El Monte
notes: San Gabriel Valley industrial center. Manufacturing, food processing, auto salvage. Rio Hondo and San Gabriel River confluence area.

name: West Covina
notes: Suburban city in San Gabriel Valley. Mostly residential and commercial with some light industrial.

name: Whittier
notes: Eastern LA County. Oil production history. Mix of residential, commercial, and some industrial.

name: Pico Rivera
notes: San Gabriel River and Rio Hondo confluence. Light manufacturing and warehousing. Whittier Narrows Dam downstream.

name: Norwalk
notes: Southeast LA County. Mix of residential, commercial, and light industrial. LA County government offices.

name: Cerritos
notes: Primarily commercial (Cerritos Auto Square) and residential. Some light industrial. Near Orange County border.

name: Azusa
notes: Foothill city. Mining and aggregate operations. Azusa Rock quarry. San Gabriel River canyon.

name: Arcadia
notes: Santa Anita racetrack. Primarily residential. Santa Anita Wash is a tributary to the San Gabriel River.

name: Monrovia
notes: Foothill city. Light industrial in southern portion. Old Town commercial district. Sawpit Wash drains to San Gabriel River.

name: Pomona
notes: Eastern LA County. Significant industrial base. Cal Poly Pomona. Fairplex. San Jose Creek tributary to San Gabriel River.

name: Downey
notes: Former aerospace hub (Rockwell/Boeing). Mix of residential, commercial, and light industrial. Rio Hondo along western boundary.

name: Bell
notes: Small, dense industrial and residential city. LA River runs along western boundary. Scrap yards and light manufacturing.

name: Maywood
notes: Smallest city in LA County by area. Dense residential with some light industrial. Adjacent to Vernon and Commerce.

name: South Gate
notes: Former steel and auto manufacturing hub. LA River runs through city. Warehousing and light industrial remain.

name: Lynwood
notes: Residential and commercial. Some light industrial along LA River corridor. Adjacent to Compton.

name: Paramount
notes: Light industrial and residential. Compton Creek runs through city. Near Compton and Lynwood.

name: Bellflower
notes: Suburban residential with some commercial. Southeast LA County. Near Lakewood and Cerritos.

name: Lakewood
notes: Planned community. Primarily residential. Some commercial. Near Long Beach. Lakewood contract city model.

name: Signal Hill
primary_waterbodies: Long Beach Harbor / San Pedro Bay
notes: Small city surrounded by Long Beach. Active oil production. Historic oil derricks. Industrial and commercial.

name: Rancho Palos Verdes
notes: Coastal bluff city. Primarily residential. Coastal zone applies. Portuguese Bend landslide area. Limited industrial.

name: Calabasas
notes: Western LA County. Malibu Creek watershed. Santa Monica Mountains. Primarily residential and commercial. Wildlife corridor.

name: Malibu
notes: Coastal city. Entire city in coastal zone. Malibu Creek watershed. Septic system issues. Very limited industrial. Coastal Commission jurisdiction.

name: Lancaster
notes: High desert city. Antelope Valley AQMD jurisdiction (NOT SCAQMD). Lahontan RWQCB Region 6. Aerospace and solar energy.

name: Palmdale
notes: High desert city. Antelope Valley AQMD jurisdiction (NOT SCAQMD). Lahontan RWQCB Region 6. Lockheed Martin/Northrop Grumman aerospace. Edwards AFB nearby.

name: Santa Clarita
notes: Northern LA County. Santa Clara River watershed. Valencia, Newhall, Saugus, Canyon Country communities. Film industry and light manufacturing.

name: East Rancho Dominguez
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Formerly known as East Compton. Industrial and residential mix. Dominguez Channel area.

name: Willowbrook
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Compton Creek corridor. Residential with some commercial. Martin Luther King Jr. Community Hospital.

name: Florence-Firestone
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Dense residential and commercial. Adjacent to Huntington Park and South Gate.

name: Walnut Park
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Small, dense residential area between Huntington Park and South Gate.

name: West Athens
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Residential with some commercial. Near Inglewood and Hawthorne.

name: Westmont
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Residential and commercial. Adjacent to West Athens. Near the 110 freeway.

name: Altadena
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Foothills above Pasadena. Eaton Wash and Arroyo Seco tributaries to LA River. Residential with some commercial.

name: La Crescenta-Montrose
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Foothill area near Glendale. Verdugo Wash drains to LA River. Primarily residential.

name: Hacienda Heights
notes: Unincorporated LA County community in San Gabriel Valley. Hsi Lai Temple. Primarily residential with some commercial.

name: Rowland Heights
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Eastern San Gabriel Valley. Primarily residential and commercial.

name: Avocado Heights
notes: Unincorporated LA County community. Near La Puente and West Covina. Residential with some industrial along major roads.
